context: A program coordinator used a personal AI tool to summarize non-sensitive public workshop comments because the approved survey tool lacked bulk summarization.
job_to_be_done: Summarize recurring themes from workshop feedback before a staff planning meeting.
why_approved_tools_failed: Approved survey export was hard to read quickly
Deadline pressure pushed staff toward an available personal tool
data_touched: Public workshop comments with names removed
No health, legal, financial, student, client, or account records in this synthetic example
risk_exposure: Staff may repeat this pattern with more sensitive data later
No shared record of what was pasted or what the tool retained
Policy looked like a ban but did not offer a workable alternative
safer_workaround: Use redacted CSV with no names/contact details
Summarize locally or with approved workspace tool only
Escalate if comments include private or sensitive details
non_punitive_next_step: Treat this as a workflow gap: define a safe comment-summary path and update the micro-policy before warning staff.
do_not_claim: This is not permission to upload confidential data
This is not a compliance clearance
This is not an employee discipline record
boundary_note: Local non-punitive triage aid only. It should surface unmet workflow demand and safer options, not encourage evasion or normalize private-data uploads.
